label: Newport Jazz Festival: The Ellington Saga, Part II

quotes.speaker: Pug Horton
quotes.text: Yeah. And we laugh a little bit about that you know. But we know where we’re going and what we’re going to do, and we like to think of music as being theatre. When Bob was director of the Smithsonian, we put on a theatrical performance. It began with me singing a cappella “Back Water Blues,” and then went into “Mournful Serenade.” On a darkened stage, Bob would walk out onto a darkened stage and talk about what is jazz, and then would build. And we would do an hour and a half of the whole history of jazz, starting with Bessie Smith and Eva Taylor and we’d start the second half with Duke Ellington and I would do the Ivy Anderson. That’s what I did with Bob. Our first gig together was at Carnegie Hall and he asked me to do the music of Ivy Anderson. I said she wasn’t really somebody that I had listened to a great deal, and I thought thank you very much, it was nice of him to ask but I wouldn’t bother. But I digress on that. I did tell him that I didn’t want to do it but he persuaded me that I should. But in any case we © Fillius Jazz Archive -10-
quotes.caption: Pug Horton recalls performing Ivy Anderson's music with Bob
quotes.doc_title: Pug Horton interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 22, 1998
